title: smilax umbellata
intro: Smilax umbellata, also known as the bignay or small-fruit catbrier, is a species of flowering plant in the Smilacaceae family, characterized by its woody vine habit and small, purple berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Smilax umbellata include potential antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, as well as the ability to regulate blood sugar levels and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of Smilax umbellata can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions.
